title: Hockey's Penalty Box Champion
story: Hockey player Randy Holt skated into the record books, but not for scoring goals. On March 27, 1982, Holt racked up an astounding 34 penalty minutes in a single game for the Washington Capitals. That's like spending over half the game in the penalty box! title: Earth's Spin Slows as Ice Melts
story: Our planet is spinning slower! Scientists confirmed on March 27, 2024, that melting ice caps are changing Earth's rotation. story: Francis II Rákóczi roared into the world on March 27, 1676. Born in Royal Hungary, this noble baby would grow up to become a fierce leader. Rákóczi led a bold uprising against the powerful Habsburg rulers. He fought bravely to free his people from foreign control.

follow_up_question: Can you name another historical figure who led an uprising for freedom?

title: A Baseball Legend's First Pitch
story: Batter up! Miller Huggins stepped onto life's diamond in 1878. Born on March 27 in Cincinnati, Ohio, he grew up to become a baseball mastermind. Huggins led the New York Yankees to incredible victories, including three World Series wins. His talent for managing players made him a true champion of America's favorite pastime.
activity_question: What team did Miller Huggins lead to victory?
bonus_fact: Miller Huggins was so good at his job, he was inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame!
